title: Sleep monitoring and tracking system and method thereof
abstract: A sleep monitoring and tracking system (100) is provided. The sleep monitoring and tracking system (100) comprising a sensing device (102) for measuring sleep related data of an occupant and a server (106) communicatively coupled to the sensing device (102) via a communication network. The sensing device (102) comprises a set of sensor units (110) configured to sense bedtime-related data of the occupant and a transmitter unit for transmitting occupancy and/or the bedtime-related data. The server (106) comprises a communication module (126) for receiving bedtime-related data of the occupant from the sensing device (102), a processor (124) for processing the bedtime-related data of the occupant to generate sleep related data for the occupant, a memory (122, 128) for storing sleep related data of the occupant and a User Interface (130) (UI) for providing display of sleep related data of the occupant. A method of analyzing sleep data and identifying sleep pattern and sleep stages namely light sleep, deep sleep and REM sleep of a user using the sleep monitoring and tracking system (100) is also described.
claims: Claims C LAIMS  I/We claim: 1. A sleep monitoring and tracking system (100) comprising: a sensing device (102) for measuring sleep related data of an occupant, the sensing device (102) comprising: a set of sensor units (110) configured to sense bedti me- related data of the occupant; and a transmitter unit (112) for transmitting occupancy and/or bedti me- related data; a server (106) communicatively coupled to the sensing device (102) via a communication network, the server (106) comprising: a communication module (126) for receiving bedti me- related data of the occupant from the sensing device (102) via a communication network (125); and a processor (124) for processing the bedti me- related data of the occupant to generate sleep related data for the occupant 2. T he system of cl ai m 1 , compri si ng a memory ( 122, 128) for stori ng bedti me rel ated data and/or sleep related data of the occupant. 3. The system of claim 1, comprising a User Interface (130) (U I) for facilitating display of sleep related data of the occupant 4. The system of claim 1, wherein the sensing device (102) comprises multiple sets of sensor units (110) for sensing sleep related variables for each occupant 5. The system of claim 4, wherein each set of sensor units (110) comprises an array of Piezoelectric Poly V inyl i dene Fluoride (PV DF) sensors. 6. The system of claim 1,wherein the communication module (126) comprises a receiver module and a transmitter module, the receiver module being configured to receive occupancy and/or bedti me- rel ated data from the transmitter unit (112) of the sensing device (102) via the communication network. 7. The system of claim 1, comprising a communication device (108) capable of communicating with the server (106) via the communication network (125), the communication device (108) adapted for accessing and displaying the sleep data of a selected user using an authorization code. 8. The system of claim 1, wherein the communication network (125) includes Internet, cloud networks, local area networks (LAN), wide area networks (WAN), virtual private networks (V PN), wireless networks (using 802.11), and/or cellular networks (using 3G and/or LTE). 9. A method of analyzing sleep data and identifying sleep pattern of a user using the sleep monitoring and tracking system (100), the method comprising: operating in a low power mode (402); checking if the user is on the mattress/bed (404); measuring one or more sensor units (110) in a sensing device (102) that are active (406); transmitting the data from the sensing device (102) to a server (106) (408); dividing the sleep data corresponding to the user into a plurality of sub intervals (410); classifying each sub interval in terms of sleep stages based on the features extracted from the sleep data in each time interval, wherein the stages are defined by specific frequency contents (412); categorizing the sleep stages into light sleep, deep sleep and Rapid Eye Movement (RE M) sleep, each of the sleep stages being defined by selected frequency contents (414); and displaying the processed sleep data (416). 10. A computer program product storing instructions which when executed by a processor (124) cause the processor (124) to perform the steps of the method claimed in claim 9.
